hypothesis:
Based on the paper's identification of stable Li-ion migration channel size as a key descriptor for Si-anode stability, a novel hypothesis extending beyond their work would be:

**Hypothesis:** Incorporating a gradient composition of alloying elements (X) within Si-based anodes—with higher X concentration at particle surfaces and lower concentration in the core—will decouple the benefits of stable ion channels (provided by the X-rich shell) from maximum gravimetric capacity (preserved by the Si-rich core), thereby achieving both >2500 mAh/g capacity and <15% channel radius variation across full lithiation cycles, surpassing the performance limits of homogeneous Si-X alloys identified in this study.
